title: Test whether ALL of a source's destinations fall inside a corporate CIDR via incidrlist
category: investigation
dataset: gx_10_conn

- comp
- alter
- sort
embedding_model: text-embedding-004

---
# Test whether ALL of a source's destinations fall inside a corporate CIDR via incidrlist

**Dataset**: `gx_10_conn`

Answers a per-source containment question useful for spotting hosts that only ever talk internally versus those reaching out. After restricting to IPv4 on both ends, `comp values(dst_ip) by src_ip` collects each source's distinct destination set, `arraystring(dsts, ",")` flattens it into a comma-separated string, and `incidrlist(dst_csv, "10.0.0.0/8")` returns true only when EVERY address in the list is inside the given CIDR. A source whose `all_dst_private` is false has at least one destination outside the corp range and is worth a closer look. Gotcha: `incidrlist` takes a delimited string (not an array), so the `values()` aggregate must be passed through `arraystring` first; it is the all-match counterpart to the any-match `incidr`.

```sql
dataset = gx_10_conn
| filter is_ipv4(src_ip) = true and is_ipv4(dst_ip) = true
| comp values(dst_ip) as dsts by src_ip
| alter dst_csv = arraystring(dsts, ",")
| alter all_dst_private = incidrlist(dst_csv, "10.0.0.0/8")
| fields src_ip, dst_csv, all_dst_private
| sort asc src_ip
```
